slides.title: Entertainment and Media
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 6 English, Unit 8: Entertainment and Media. Welcome to Unit 8: Entertainment and Media. In today's world, entertainment is everywhere. We watch films and documentaries on screens, listen to podcasts and radio shows, follow stories through books and graphic novels, and even create our own content. But how much do we think about the media we consume? How does a film tell a story? What makes a documentary different from a drama? Why do some commercials stay in our minds long after we have seen them? In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand texts about entertainment and media, including film, television, and storytelling traditions
Use participle adjectives correctly, choosing between the -ed form (how someone feels) and the -ing form (what causes the feeling)
Form sentences in the past simple passive to describe events in film and history (e.g. "The documentary was filmed in Mogadishu")
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand texts about entertainment and media, including film, television, and storytelling traditions. you will also use participle adjectives correctly, choosing between the -ed form (how someone feels) and the -ing form (what causes the feeling). you will also form sentences in the past simple passive to describe events in film and history (e.g. "the documentary was filmed in mogadishu"). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: ritual, disciple, eternal, revival, pagan, Catholic. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Entertainment and Media: source text 1 and Entertainment and Media: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Participle Adjectives (-ed and -ing)
bullets: What the rule means and why it matters Participle adjectives are words that come from verbs and work as describing words (adjectives). They help us add...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Participle Adjectives (-ed and -ing). What the rule means and why it matters Participle adjectives are words that come from verbs and work as describing words (adjectives). They help us add detail and emotion to our sentences. Understanding the difference between the -ed form and the -ing form is one of the most important grammar skills at this level, because getting it wrong can change the meaning of your sentence completely. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
